solution.p: There are at least two methods to reduce the fraction \displaystyle{ \frac{2}{20} }.
Method 1:We find a number that divides into both the numerator 2, and the denominator 20.
Check the first few prime numbers one by one: 2, 3, 5, 7, \ldots
In this case, 2 goes into both the numerator and denominator of \displaystyle{ \frac{2}{20} }. We divide 2 into both numbers, and we have:
Next, check again whether any prime number divides into both the numerator and denominator. We need to keep trying until no prime numbers divide into both numbers.
In this case, \displaystyle{\frac{1}{10}} is the final answer.
A second method to reduce fraction is to prime factor both the numerator and the denominator, and then cancel out factors in pairs: one from the numerator and one from the denominator.
Notice that when the numerators only prime factor, 2, is canceled, we have to leave a 1 in the numerator.
